title: Early versus deferred immunotherapy in urothelial carcinoma: a review of evidence and post-progression survival.
abstract: Metastatic urothelial carcinoma has undergone a major therapeutic transition, with first-line immune checkpoint inhibitor-based combinations now established as standard care. In this context, the key clinical question is no longer whether early immunotherapy should be used, but how evidence from historical deferred approaches, switch-maintenance, and contemporary upfront regimens can be interpreted through the lens of post-progression survival (PPS), a major determinant of overall survival (OS).
